== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4012405000982 == Commons category (P373) == * Kohaku-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Torakashiwa Shrine|lt=Torakashiwa Shrine|WD=Q135039730|1=Torakashiwa Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都調布市佐須町1-14-3', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都青梅市根ケ布', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kohaku-jinja}} [[JA:虎狛神社 (調布市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72707416}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kohaku Shrine | image = [[File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu).jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}} Sasu-cho 1-14-3 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = 588 (trad.) | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with mise-dana style | annual_festival = First Sunday of October | map = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Kohaku Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kohaku Shrine''' (こはくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine near {{ill|lt=Jindaiji|Q500736|WD=Q500736|1=Jindai-ji (Tokyo)}} in Sasu-cho, {{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It enshrines Otoshi Mioya no Kami. It is a candidate for the Engishiki shrine "Kohaku Shrine" of Tama District, Musashi Province, and was formerly ranked as a gosha and tutelary shrine of old Sasu Village. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in 588 by enshrining Otoshi Mioya no Kami. {{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}} Traditions connected with Jindaiji state that Manko Shonin's grandparents were worshiped here as guardian deities of Jindaiji.''Jindaiji Engi''. The shrine name "Kohaku" is linked to stories about his grandmother "Tora" and grandfather "Ukon" and the old place-name "Komano-no-sato." In later centuries, Gion-ji (Kohakusan Nikko-in), founded by Manko Shonin, served as the shrine's betto temple until the Edo period. ; Shrine rank * {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} - designated {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.''Kitatama Jinjashi'' (Kitatama Shinto Youth Association Murasaki-kai, 1976). === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Kamioichihime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} ; Co-enshrined deity * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}On-site origin guide board (installed January 2015). === Cultural properties === ; Chofu City designated tangible cultural properties * Honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Built in 1683; repaired in 1815. One-bay nagare-zukuri mise-dana style. Original thatched roof replaced with copper sheeting.Chofu City Board of Education signboard for Kohaku Shrine Honden and three munafuda. * Three munafuda attached to the honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Include inscriptions from 750, 1683, and 1815. ; Other items * Stone torii (built 1828) * Monument "Komae-go Sasu-mura Kohaku Jinja no Hi" * Komainu (built 1908) * Haiden File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Hengaku.jpg|Plaque at torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Temizusha.jpg|Temizuya File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Haiden.jpg|Haiden === Outside-precinct shrines === Sasu Shinmeigu and Kashinoki Inari, once merged in 1911, were re-enshrined at their former locations in 1942. * Sasu Shinmeigu (next to Gion-ji) : Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}} : Festival: December 13 * Kashinoki Inari (southwest corner of Koka Gakuen grounds) : Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} : Festival: Hatsuuma day in February === Annual events === * Monthly festival (13th of each month except January and October) * New Year's Day festival (January 1) * Inari Hatsuuma festival (first horse day of February) * Kohaku Shrine grand festival (first Sunday of October) * Shinmeigu grand festival (December 13) * Niiname-sai (December 13) === Access === * From JR Mitaka Station, Keio Bus for Chofu Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * From Keio Chofu Station, Keio Bus for Mitaka Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * Chofu City minibuses also stop at "Kohaku Shrine." === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudokiko|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 94, Tama District 6|chapter=Sasu Village: Kohaku Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/30}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}}}} * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/155}}|publisher=Yuhodo Shoten|editor=Nagatoshi Saito|volume=2|series=Yuhodo Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3 Tenki section: Kohaku Shrine|pages=300-301|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} * {{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.chofu.tokyo.jp/www/contents/1176118969587/index.html|title=Monument of Kohaku Shrine in Sasu Village, Komae-go|website=Chofu City website|accessdate=2021-1-13|date=2004-4-24|ref={{sfnref|調布市ホームページ}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine|Q24867966|WD=Q24867966|1=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:調布市の歴史]] [[Category:調布市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4012405000982 == Commons category (P373) == * Kohaku-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Torakashiwa Shrine|lt=Torakashiwa Shrine|WD=Q135039730|1=Torakashiwa Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都調布市佐須町1-14-3', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都青梅市根ケ布', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kohaku-jinja}} [[JA:虎狛神社 (調布市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72707416}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kohaku Shrine | image = [[File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu).jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}} Sasu-cho 1-14-3 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = 588 (trad.) | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with mise-dana style | annual_festival = First Sunday of October | map = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Kohaku Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kohaku Shrine''' (こはくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine near {{ill|lt=Jindaiji|Q500736|WD=Q500736|1=Jindai-ji (Tokyo)}} in Sasu-cho, {{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It enshrines Otoshi Mioya no Kami. It is a candidate for the Engishiki shrine "Kohaku Shrine" of Tama District, Musashi Province, and was formerly ranked as a gosha and tutelary shrine of old Sasu Village. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in 588 by enshrining Otoshi Mioya no Kami. {{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}} Traditions connected with Jindaiji state that Manko Shonin's grandparents were worshiped here as guardian deities of Jindaiji.''Jindaiji Engi''. The shrine name "Kohaku" is linked to stories about his grandmother "Tora" and grandfather "Ukon" and the old place-name "Komano-no-sato." In later centuries, Gion-ji (Kohakusan Nikko-in), founded by Manko Shonin, served as the shrine's betto temple until the Edo period. ; Shrine rank * {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} - designated {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.''Kitatama Jinjashi'' (Kitatama Shinto Youth Association Murasaki-kai, 1976). === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Kamioichihime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} ; Co-enshrined deity * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}On-site origin guide board (installed January 2015). === Cultural properties === ; Chofu City designated tangible cultural properties * Honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Built in 1683; repaired in 1815. One-bay nagare-zukuri mise-dana style. Original thatched roof replaced with copper sheeting.Chofu City Board of Education signboard for Kohaku Shrine Honden and three munafuda. * Three munafuda attached to the honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Include inscriptions from 750, 1683, and 1815. ; Other items * Stone torii (built 1828) * Monument "Komae-go Sasu-mura Kohaku Jinja no Hi" * Komainu (built 1908) * Haiden File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Hengaku.jpg|Plaque at torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Temizusha.jpg|Temizuya File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Haiden.jpg|Haiden === Outside-precinct shrines === Sasu Shinmeigu and Kashinoki Inari, once merged in 1911, were re-enshrined at their former locations in 1942. * Sasu Shinmeigu (next to Gion-ji) : Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}} : Festival: December 13 * Kashinoki Inari (southwest corner of Koka Gakuen grounds) : Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} : Festival: Hatsuuma day in February === Annual events === * Monthly festival (13th of each month except January and October) * New Year's Day festival (January 1) * Inari Hatsuuma festival (first horse day of February) * Kohaku Shrine grand festival (first Sunday of October) * Shinmeigu grand festival (December 13) * Niiname-sai (December 13) === Access === * From JR Mitaka Station, Keio Bus for Chofu Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * From Keio Chofu Station, Keio Bus for Mitaka Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * Chofu City minibuses also stop at "Kohaku Shrine." === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudokiko|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 94, Tama District 6|chapter=Sasu Village: Kohaku Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/30}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}}}} * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/155}}|publisher=Yuhodo Shoten|editor=Nagatoshi Saito|volume=2|series=Yuhodo Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3 Tenki section: Kohaku Shrine|pages=300-301|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} * {{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.chofu.tokyo.jp/www/contents/1176118969587/index.html|title=Monument of Kohaku Shrine in Sasu Village, Komae-go|website=Chofu City website|accessdate=2021-1-13|date=2004-4-24|ref={{sfnref|調布市ホームページ}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine|Q24867966|WD=Q24867966|1=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:調布市の歴史]] [[Category:調布市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

'東京都調布市佐須町1-14-3', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都青梅市根ケ布', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kohaku-jinja}} [[JA:虎狛神社 (調布市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72707416}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kohaku Shrine | image = [[File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu).jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}} Sasu-cho 1-14-3 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = 588 (trad.) | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with mise-dana style | annual_festival = First Sunday of October | map = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Kohaku Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kohaku Shrine''' (こはくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine near {{ill|lt=Jindaiji|Q500736|WD=Q500736|1=Jindai-ji (Tokyo)}} in Sasu-cho, {{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It enshrines Otoshi Mioya no Kami. It is a candidate for the Engishiki shrine "Kohaku Shrine" of Tama District, Musashi Province, and was formerly ranked as a gosha and tutelary shrine of old Sasu Village. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in 588 by enshrining Otoshi Mioya no Kami. {{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}} Traditions connected with Jindaiji state that Manko Shonin's grandparents were worshiped here as guardian deities of Jindaiji.''Jindaiji Engi''. The shrine name "Kohaku" is linked to stories about his grandmother "Tora" and grandfather "Ukon" and the old place-name "Komano-no-sato." In later centuries, Gion-ji (Kohakusan Nikko-in), founded by Manko Shonin, served as the shrine's betto temple until the Edo period. ; Shrine rank * {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} - designated {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.''Kitatama Jinjashi'' (Kitatama Shinto Youth Association Murasaki-kai, 1976). === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Kamioichihime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} ; Co-enshrined deity * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}On-site origin guide board (installed January 2015). === Cultural properties === ; Chofu City designated tangible cultural properties * Honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Built in 1683; repaired in 1815. One-bay nagare-zukuri mise-dana style. Original thatched roof replaced with copper sheeting.Chofu City Board of Education signboard for Kohaku Shrine Honden and three munafuda. * Three munafuda attached to the honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Include inscriptions from 750, 1683, and 1815. ; Other items * Stone torii (built 1828) * Monument "Komae-go Sasu-mura Kohaku Jinja no Hi" * Komainu (built 1908) * Haiden File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Hengaku.jpg|Plaque at torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Temizusha.jpg|Temizuya File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Haiden.jpg|Haiden === Outside-precinct shrines === Sasu Shinmeigu and Kashinoki Inari, once merged in 1911, were re-enshrined at their former locations in 1942. * Sasu Shinmeigu (next to Gion-ji) : Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}} : Festival: December 13 * Kashinoki Inari (southwest corner of Koka Gakuen grounds) : Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} : Festival: Hatsuuma day in February === Annual events === * Monthly festival (13th of each month except January and October) * New Year's Day festival (January 1) * Inari Hatsuuma festival (first horse day of February) * Kohaku Shrine grand festival (first Sunday of October) * Shinmeigu grand festival (December 13) * Niiname-sai (December 13) === Access === * From JR Mitaka Station, Keio Bus for Chofu Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * From Keio Chofu Station, Keio Bus for Mitaka Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * Chofu City minibuses also stop at "Kohaku Shrine." === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudokiko|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 94, Tama District 6|chapter=Sasu Village: Kohaku Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/30}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}}}} * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/155}}|publisher=Yuhodo Shoten|editor=Nagatoshi Saito|volume=2|series=Yuhodo Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3 Tenki section: Kohaku Shrine|pages=300-301|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} * {{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.chofu.tokyo.jp/www/contents/1176118969587/index.html|title=Monument of Kohaku Shrine in Sasu Village, Komae-go|website=Chofu City website|accessdate=2021-1-13|date=2004-4-24|ref={{sfnref|調布市ホームページ}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine|Q24867966|WD=Q24867966|1=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:調布市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chōfu, Tokyo]][[Category:qq]]

'東京都調布市佐須町1-14-3', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都青梅市根ケ布', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kohaku-jinja}} [[JA:虎狛神社 (調布市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72707416}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kohaku Shrine | image = [[File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu).jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}} Sasu-cho 1-14-3 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = 588 (trad.) | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with mise-dana style | annual_festival = First Sunday of October | map = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Kohaku Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kohaku Shrine''' (こはくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine near {{ill|lt=Jindaiji|Q500736|WD=Q500736|1=Jindai-ji (Tokyo)}} in Sasu-cho, {{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It enshrines Otoshi Mioya no Kami. It is a candidate for the Engishiki shrine "Kohaku Shrine" of Tama District, Musashi Province, and was formerly ranked as a gosha and tutelary shrine of old Sasu Village. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in 588 by enshrining Otoshi Mioya no Kami. {{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}} Traditions connected with Jindaiji state that Manko Shonin's grandparents were worshiped here as guardian deities of Jindaiji.''Jindaiji Engi''. The shrine name "Kohaku" is linked to stories about his grandmother "Tora" and grandfather "Ukon" and the old place-name "Komano-no-sato." In later centuries, Gion-ji (Kohakusan Nikko-in), founded by Manko Shonin, served as the shrine's betto temple until the Edo period. ; Shrine rank * {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} - designated {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.''Kitatama Jinjashi'' (Kitatama Shinto Youth Association Murasaki-kai, 1976). === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Kamioichihime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} ; Co-enshrined deity * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}On-site origin guide board (installed January 2015). === Cultural properties === ; Chofu City designated tangible cultural properties * Honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Built in 1683; repaired in 1815. One-bay nagare-zukuri mise-dana style. Original thatched roof replaced with copper sheeting.Chofu City Board of Education signboard for Kohaku Shrine Honden and three munafuda. * Three munafuda attached to the honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Include inscriptions from 750, 1683, and 1815. ; Other items * Stone torii (built 1828) * Monument "Komae-go Sasu-mura Kohaku Jinja no Hi" * Komainu (built 1908) * Haiden File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Hengaku.jpg|Plaque at torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Temizusha.jpg|Temizuya File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Haiden.jpg|Haiden === Outside-precinct shrines === Sasu Shinmeigu and Kashinoki Inari, once merged in 1911, were re-enshrined at their former locations in 1942. * Sasu Shinmeigu (next to Gion-ji) : Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}} : Festival: December 13 * Kashinoki Inari (southwest corner of Koka Gakuen grounds) : Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} : Festival: Hatsuuma day in February === Annual events === * Monthly festival (13th of each month except January and October) * New Year's Day festival (January 1) * Inari Hatsuuma festival (first horse day of February) * Kohaku Shrine grand festival (first Sunday of October) * Shinmeigu grand festival (December 13) * Niiname-sai (December 13) === Access === * From JR Mitaka Station, Keio Bus for Chofu Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * From Keio Chofu Station, Keio Bus for Mitaka Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * Chofu City minibuses also stop at "Kohaku Shrine." === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudokiko|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 94, Tama District 6|chapter=Sasu Village: Kohaku Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/30}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}}}} * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/155}}|publisher=Yuhodo Shoten|editor=Nagatoshi Saito|volume=2|series=Yuhodo Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3 Tenki section: Kohaku Shrine|pages=300-301|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} * {{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.chofu.tokyo.jp/www/contents/1176118969587/index.html|title=Monument of Kohaku Shrine in Sasu Village, Komae-go|website=Chofu City website|accessdate=2021-1-13|date=2004-4-24|ref={{sfnref|調布市ホームページ}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine|Q24867966|WD=Q24867966|1=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:調布市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chōfu, Tokyo]]

'東京都調布市佐須町1-14-3', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都青梅市根ケ布', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kohaku-jinja}} [[JA:虎狛神社 (調布市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72707416}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kohaku Shrine | image = [[File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu).jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}} Sasu-cho 1-14-3 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = 588 (trad.) | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with mise-dana style | annual_festival = First Sunday of October | map = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Kohaku Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kohaku Shrine''' (こはくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine near {{ill|lt=Jindaiji|Q500736|WD=Q500736|1=Jindai-ji (Tokyo)}} in Sasu-cho, {{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|WD=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It enshrines Otoshi Mioya no Kami. It is a candidate for the Engishiki shrine "Kohaku Shrine" of Tama District, Musashi Province, and was formerly ranked as a gosha and tutelary shrine of old Sasu Village. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in 588 by enshrining Otoshi Mioya no Kami. {{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}} Traditions connected with Jindaiji state that Manko Shonin's grandparents were worshiped here as guardian deities of Jindaiji.''Jindaiji Engi''. The shrine name "Kohaku" is linked to stories about his grandmother "Tora" and grandfather "Ukon" and the old place-name "Komano-no-sato." In later centuries, Gion-ji (Kohakusan Nikko-in), founded by Manko Shonin, served as the shrine's betto temple until the Edo period. ; Shrine rank * {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} - designated {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.''Kitatama Jinjashi'' (Kitatama Shinto Youth Association Murasaki-kai, 1976). === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Kamioichihime|Q11588867|WD=Q11588867|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} ; Co-enshrined deity * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}On-site origin guide board (installed January 2015). === Cultural properties === ; Chofu City designated tangible cultural properties * Honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Built in 1683; repaired in 1815. One-bay nagare-zukuri mise-dana style. Original thatched roof replaced with copper sheeting.Chofu City Board of Education signboard for Kohaku Shrine Honden and three munafuda. * Three munafuda attached to the honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Include inscriptions from 750, 1683, and 1815. ; Other items * Stone torii (built 1828) * Monument "Komae-go Sasu-mura Kohaku Jinja no Hi" * Komainu (built 1908) * Haiden File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Hengaku.jpg|Plaque at torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Temizusha.jpg|Temizuya File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Haiden.jpg|Haiden === Outside-precinct shrines === Sasu Shinmeigu and Kashinoki Inari, once merged in 1911, were re-enshrined at their former locations in 1942. * Sasu Shinmeigu (next to Gion-ji) : Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}} : Festival: December 13 * Kashinoki Inari (southwest corner of Koka Gakuen grounds) : Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} : Festival: Hatsuuma day in February === Annual events === * Monthly festival (13th of each month except January and October) * New Year's Day festival (January 1) * Inari Hatsuuma festival (first horse day of February) * Kohaku Shrine grand festival (first Sunday of October) * Shinmeigu grand festival (December 13) * Niiname-sai (December 13) === Access === * From JR Mitaka Station, Keio Bus for Chofu Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * From Keio Chofu Station, Keio Bus for Mitaka Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * Chofu City minibuses also stop at "Kohaku Shrine." === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudokiko|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 94, Tama District 6|chapter=Sasu Village: Kohaku Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/30}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}}}} * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|WD=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/155}}|publisher=Yuhodo Shoten|editor=Nagatoshi Saito|volume=2|series=Yuhodo Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3 Tenki section: Kohaku Shrine|pages=300-301|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} * {{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.chofu.tokyo.jp/www/contents/1176118969587/index.html|title=Monument of Kohaku Shrine in Sasu Village, Komae-go|website=Chofu City website|accessdate=2021-1-13|date=2004-4-24|ref={{sfnref|調布市ホームページ}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine|Q24867966|WD=Q24867966|1=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:調布市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chōfu, Tokyo]]

'東京都調布市佐須町1-14-3', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都青梅市根ケ布', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kohaku-jinja}} [[JA:虎狛神社 (調布市)]] {{wikidata link|Q72707416}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kohaku Shrine | image = [[File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu).jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|qid=Q210667|1=Chōfu}} Sasu-cho 1-14-3 | coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = 588 (trad.) | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with mise-dana style | annual_festival = First Sunday of October | map = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Kohaku Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kohaku Shrine''' (こはくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine near {{ill|lt=Jindaiji|Q500736|qid=Q500736|1=Jindai-ji (Tokyo)}} in Sasu-cho, {{ill|lt=Chofu|Q210667|qid=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It enshrines Otoshi Mioya no Kami. It is a candidate for the Engishiki shrine "Kohaku Shrine" of Tama District, Musashi Province, and was formerly ranked as a gosha and tutelary shrine of old Sasu Village. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in 588 by enshrining Otoshi Mioya no Kami. {{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}} Traditions connected with Jindaiji state that Manko Shonin's grandparents were worshiped here as guardian deities of Jindaiji.''Jindaiji Engi''. The shrine name "Kohaku" is linked to stories about his grandmother "Tora" and grandfather "Ukon" and the old place-name "Komano-no-sato." In later centuries, Gion-ji (Kohakusan Nikko-in), founded by Manko Shonin, served as the shrine's betto temple until the Edo period. ; Shrine rank * {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} - designated {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.''Kitatama Jinjashi'' (Kitatama Shinto Youth Association Murasaki-kai, 1976). === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Kamioichihime|Q11588867|qid=Q11588867|lt=Otoshi Mioya no Kami|1=Kamuo Ichihime}} ; Co-enshrined deity * {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}On-site origin guide board (installed January 2015). === Cultural properties === ; Chofu City designated tangible cultural properties * Honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Built in 1683; repaired in 1815. One-bay nagare-zukuri mise-dana style. Original thatched roof replaced with copper sheeting.Chofu City Board of Education signboard for Kohaku Shrine Honden and three munafuda. * Three munafuda attached to the honden (designated March 30, 2012) : Include inscriptions from 750, 1683, and 1815. ; Other items * Stone torii (built 1828) * Monument "Komae-go Sasu-mura Kohaku Jinja no Hi" * Komainu (built 1908) * Haiden File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Hengaku.jpg|Plaque at torii File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Temizusha.jpg|Temizuya File:Kohaku-jinja(Chofu) Haiden.jpg|Haiden === Outside-precinct shrines === Sasu Shinmeigu and Kashinoki Inari, once merged in 1911, were re-enshrined at their former locations in 1942. * Sasu Shinmeigu (next to Gion-ji) : Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}} : Festival: December 13 * Kashinoki Inari (southwest corner of Koka Gakuen grounds) : Deity: {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} : Festival: Hatsuuma day in February === Annual events === * Monthly festival (13th of each month except January and October) * New Year's Day festival (January 1) * Inari Hatsuuma festival (first horse day of February) * Kohaku Shrine grand festival (first Sunday of October) * Shinmeigu grand festival (December 13) * Niiname-sai (December 13) === Access === * From JR Mitaka Station, Keio Bus for Chofu Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * From Keio Chofu Station, Keio Bus for Mitaka Station; get off at "Sasu," about 5 minutes on foot. * Chofu City minibuses also stop at "Kohaku Shrine." === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudokiko|Q11503943|qid=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 94, Tama District 6|chapter=Sasu Village: Kohaku Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/30}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 佐須村}}}} * {{cite book|ja|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|qid=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/155}}|publisher=Yuhodo Shoten|editor=Nagatoshi Saito|volume=2|series=Yuhodo Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3 Tenki section: Kohaku Shrine|pages=300-301|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} * {{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.chofu.tokyo.jp/www/contents/1176118969587/index.html|title=Monument of Kohaku Shrine in Sasu Village, Komae-go|website=Chofu City website|accessdate=2021-1-13|date=2004-4-24|ref={{sfnref|調布市ホームページ}}}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine|Q24867966|qid=Q24867966|1=Kohaku Shrine Black Pine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kohaku Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:調布市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chōfu, Tokyo]]
